    1184             :

    1203           0 :             CPLAddXMLChild(psTree, psMD);

    1241             :         }

    1266             :         }
    1267             :     }
    1268           9 :     if( nCountCandidateVars > 1 )
    1269             :     {
    1270           1 :         if( bVerboseError )

    1274           1 :                  pszDimName);
    1275             :         }

    1277           1 :         return -1;
    1278             :     }

    1992        4913 :     if( nd > 3 )
    1993             :     {
    1994         160 :         int Sum = -1;

    1998         320 :             if( i != nd - 2 - 1 )

    2000         160 :                 Sum = 1;
    2001         320 :                 for( int j = i + 1; j < nd - 2; j++ )
    2002             :                 {
    2003         160 :                     Sum *= panBandZLev[j];
    2004             :                 }
    2005         160 :                 start[panBandZPos[i]] = (int)((nLevel - Taken) / Sum);
    2006         160 :                 edge[panBandZPos[i]] = 1;
    2007             :             }
    2008             :             else
    2009             :             {
    2010         160 :                 start[panBandZPos[i]] = (int)((nLevel - Taken) % Sum);
    2011         160 :                 edge[panBandZPos[i]] = 1;
    2012             :             }
    2013         320 :             Taken += static_cast<int>(start[panBandZPos[i]]) * Sum;
    2014             :         }
    2015             :     }

    2017             :     // Make sure we are in data mode.

    2524             : 
    2525             :     // Get information about the file.
    2526             :     //
    2527             :     // Was this file created by the GDAL netcdf driver?
    2528             :     // Was this file created by the newer (CF-conformant) driver?
    2529             :     //
    2530             :     // 1) If GDAL netcdf metadata is set, and version >= 1.9,
    2531             :     //    it was created with the new driver
    2532             :     // 2) Else, if spatial_ref and GeoTransform are present in the
    2533             :     //    grid_mapping variable, it was created by the old driver
    2534         345 :     pszValue = FetchAttr("NC_GLOBAL", "GDAL");
    2535             : 
    2536         345 :     if( pszValue && NCDFIsGDALVersionGTE(pszValue, 1900))
    2537             :     {
    2538         243 :         bIsGdalFile = true;
    2539         243 :         bIsGdalCfFile = true;
    2540             :     }
    2541         102 :     else if( pszWKT != nullptr && pszGeoTransform != nullptr )
    2542             :     {
    2543           3 :         bIsGdalFile = true;
    2544           3 :         bIsGdalCfFile = false;
    2545             :     }
    2546             : 
    2547             :     // Set default bottom-up default value.
    2548             :     // Y axis dimension and absence of GT can modify this value.
    2549             :     // Override with Config option GDAL_NETCDF_BOTTOMUP.
    2550             : 
    2551             :     // New driver is bottom-up by default.
    2552         345 :     if( (bIsGdalFile && !bIsGdalCfFile) || bSwitchedXY )
    2553           5 :         poDS->bBottomUp = false;
    2554             :     else
    2555         340 :         poDS->bBottomUp = true;

    2925             :                         {
    2926             :                             CPLError(

    2928             :                                 "NetCDF driver import of LCC-1SP with "
    2929             :                                 "standard_parallel1 != "
    2930             :                                 "latitude_of_projection_origin "
    2931             :                                 "(which forces a computation of scale_factor) "
    2932           0 :                                 "is experimental (bug #3324)");

    2934             :                             // dfStdP1 and dfCenterLat.  Only tested for
    2935             :                             // dfStdP1=dfCenterLat and (25,26), needs more data
    2936             :                             // for testing.  Other option: use the 2SP variant -
    2937             :                             // how to compute new standard parallels?

    2939           0 :                                 (cos(dfStdP1) *

    2941           0 :                                      sin(dfStdP1))) /
    2942           0 :                                 (cos(dfCenterLat) *

    7364             :     // CF-1 Convention

    8925             : /************************************************************************/
    8926             : /*                          New functions                               */
    8927             : /************************************************************************/
    8928             : 
    8929             : /* Test for GDAL version string >= target */
    8930         243 : static bool NCDFIsGDALVersionGTE(const char *pszVersion, int nTarget)
    8931             : {
    8932             : 
    8933             :     // Valid strings are "GDAL 1.9dev, released 2011/01/18" and "GDAL 1.8.1 ".
    8934         243 :     if( pszVersion == nullptr || EQUAL(pszVersion, "") )
    8935           0 :         return false;
    8936         243 :     else if( !STARTS_WITH_CI(pszVersion, "GDAL ") )
    8937           0 :         return false;
    8938             :     // 2.0dev of 2011/12/29 has been later renamed as 1.10dev.
    8939         243 :     else if( EQUAL("GDAL 2.0dev, released 2011/12/29", pszVersion) )
    8940           0 :         return nTarget <= GDAL_COMPUTE_VERSION(1, 10, 0);
    8941         243 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszVersion, "GDAL 1.9dev") )
    8942           1 :         return nTarget <= 1900;
    8943         242 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszVersion, "GDAL 1.8dev") )
    8944           0 :         return nTarget <= 1800;
    8945             : 
    8946         242 :     char **papszTokens = CSLTokenizeString2(pszVersion + 5, ".", 0);
    8947             : 
    8948         242 :     int nVersions[] = {0, 0, 0, 0};
    8949         968 :     for( int iToken = 0;
    8950         968 :          papszTokens && iToken < 4 && papszTokens[iToken];
    8951             :          iToken++ )
    8952             :     {
    8953         726 :         nVersions[iToken] = atoi(papszTokens[iToken]);
    8954         726 :         if( nVersions[iToken] < 0 )
    8955           0 :             nVersions[iToken] = 0;
    8956         726 :         else if( nVersions[iToken] > 99 )

    9069             : 
    9070             : /* Write any needed projection attributes *
    9071             :  * poPROJCS: ptr to proj crd system
    9072             :  * pszProjection: name of projection system in GDAL WKT
    9073             :  * fpImage: open NetCDF file in writing mode
    9074             :  * NCDFVarID: NetCDF Var Id of proj system we're writing in to
    9075             :  *
    9076             :  * The function first looks for the oNetcdfSRS_PP mapping object
    9077             :  * that corresponds to the input projection name. If none is found
    9078             :  * the generic mapping is used.  In the case of specific mappings,
    9079             :  * the driver looks for each attribute listed in the mapping object
    9080             :  * and then looks up the value within the OGR_SRSNode. In the case
    9081             :  * of the generic mapping, the lookup is reversed (projection params,
    9082             :  * then mapping).  For more generic code, GDAL->NETCDF
    9083             :  * mappings and the associated value are saved in std::map objects.
    9084             :  */
    9085             : 
    9086             : // NOTE: modifications by ET to combine the specific and generic mappings.
    9087             :

    9104             :     }

   10723             : // Implementation of NCDFResolveVar/Att.
   10724       12376 : static CPLErr NCDFResolveElem( int nStartGroupId,
   10725             :                                const char *pszVar, const char *pszAtt,
   10726             :                                int *pnGroupId, int *pnId, bool bMandatory )
   10727             : {
   10728       12376 :     if( !pszVar && !pszAtt )
   10729             :     {

   10733             :     }
   10734             : 
   10735       12376 :     enum {NCRM_PARENT, NCRM_WIDTH_WISE} eNCResolveMode = NCRM_PARENT;
   10736             : 
   10737       12376 :     std::queue<int> aoQueueGroupIdsToVisit;
   10738       12376 :     aoQueueGroupIdsToVisit.push(nStartGroupId);
   10739             : 
   10740       25999 :     while( !aoQueueGroupIdsToVisit.empty() )
   10741             :     {
   10742             :         // Get the first group of the FIFO queue.
   10743       12491 :         *pnGroupId = aoQueueGroupIdsToVisit.front();
   10744       12491 :         aoQueueGroupIdsToVisit.pop();
   10745             : 
   10746             :         // Look if this group contains the searched element.
   10747             :         int status;
   10748       12491 :         if( pszVar )
   10749       12240 :             status = nc_inq_varid(*pnGroupId, pszVar, pnId);

   10751         251 :             status = nc_inq_attid(*pnGroupId, NC_GLOBAL, pszAtt, pnId);
   10752             : 
   10753       12491 :         if( status == NC_NOERR )
   10754             :         {
   10755       11244 :             return CE_None;
   10756             :         }
   10757        1247 :         else if( (pszVar && status != NC_ENOTVAR) ||
   10758         247 :                  (pszAtt && status != NC_ENOTATT) )
   10759             :         {
   10760           0 :             NCDF_ERR(status);
   10761             :         }
   10762             : #ifdef NETCDF_HAS_NC4
   10763             :         // Element not found, in NC4 case we must search in other groups
   10764             :         // following the CF logic.
   10765             : 
   10766             :         // The first resolve mode consists to search on parent groups.
   10767        1247 :         if( eNCResolveMode == NCRM_PARENT )
   10768             :         {
   10769        1187 :             int nParentGroupId = -1;
   10770        1187 :             int status2 = nc_inq_grp_parent(*pnGroupId, &nParentGroupId);
   10771        1187 :             if( status2 == NC_NOERR )
   10772          63 :                 aoQueueGroupIdsToVisit.push(nParentGroupId);
   10773        1124 :             else if( status2 != NC_ENOGRP )
   10774           0 :                 NCDF_ERR(status2);
   10775        1187 :             if( pszVar )
   10776             :                 // When resolving a variable, if there is no more
   10777             :                 // parent group then we switch to width-wise search mode
   10778             :                 // starting from the latest found parent group.
   10779         940 :                 eNCResolveMode = NCRM_WIDTH_WISE;
   10780             :         }
   10781             : 
   10782             :         // The second resolve mode is a width-wise search.
   10783        1247 :         if( eNCResolveMode == NCRM_WIDTH_WISE )

   10812             : 
   10813             : // Resolve a variable name from a given starting group following the CF logic:
   10814             : // - if var name is an absolute path then directly open it
   10815             : // - first search in the starting group and its parent groups
   10816             : // - then if there is no more parent group we switch to a width-wise search
   10817             : //   mode starting from the latest found parent group.
   10818             : // The full CF logic is described here:
   10819             : // https://github.com/diwg/cf2/blob/master/group/cf2-group.adoc#scope
   10820             : // If bMandatory then print an error if resolving fails.
   10821             : // TODO: implement support of relative paths.
   10822             : // TODO: to follow strictly the CF logic, when searching for a coordinate
   10823             : //       variable, we must stop the parent search mode once the corresponding
   10824             : //       dimension is found and start the width-wise search from this group.
   10825             : // TODO: to follow strictly the CF logic, when searching in width-wise mode
   10826             : //       we should skip every groups already visited during the parent
   10827             : //       search mode (but revisiting them should have no impact so we could
   10828             : //       let as it is if it is simpler...)
   10829             : // TODO: CF specifies that the width-wise search order is "left-to-right" so
   10830             : //       maybe we must sort sibling groups alphabetically? but maybe not
   10831             : //       necessary if nc_inq_grps() already sort them?

   10833             :                               int *pnGroupId, int *pnVarId,
   10834             :                               bool bMandatory )
   10835             : {
   10836       12142 :     *pnGroupId = -1;
   10837       12142 :     *pnVarId = -1;
   10838       12142 :     int nGroupId = nStartGroupId, nVarId;
   10839       12142 :     if( pszVar[0] == '/' )
   10840             :     {
   10841             :         // This is an absolute path: we can open the var directly.
   10842             :         int nRootGroupId;
   10843           0 :         ERR_RET(NCDFGetRootGroup(nStartGroupId, &nRootGroupId));
   10844           0 :         ERR_RET(NCDFOpenSubDataset(nRootGroupId, pszVar, &nGroupId, &nVarId));
   10845             :     }
   10846             :     else
   10847             :     {
   10848             :         // We have to search the variable following the CF logic.
   10849       12142 :         ERR_RET(NCDFResolveElem(nStartGroupId, pszVar, nullptr,
   10850             :                                 &nGroupId, &nVarId, bMandatory));
   10851             :     }
   10852       11240 :     *pnGroupId = nGroupId;
   10853       11240 :     *pnVarId = nVarId;
   10854       11240 :     return CE_None;
   10855             : }
   10856             :

   10865             :                            bMandatory));

   11325             : // Get all coordinate and boundary variables full names referenced in
   11326             : // a given a NetCDF (or group) ID and its sub-groups.
   11327             : // These variables are identified in other variable's

   11331             : // absolute path).
